records.ety: [F. commodit, fr. L. commoditas. See Commode.]

defs: Convenience; accommodation; profit; benefit; advantage; interest; commodiousness.
quotes: Drawn by the commodity of a footpath.
Men may seek their own commodity, yet if this were done with injury to others, it was not to be suffered.

defs: That which affords convenience, advantage, or profit, especially in commerce, including everything movable that is bought and sold (except animals), -- goods, wares, merchandise, produce of land and manufactures, etc.
A parcel or quantity of goods.
quotes: A commodity of brown paper and old ginger.
